claim_status: Research package/update readiness-review template only; no owner review, no independent review, no release-operations review, no security review, no privacy review, no executable package manifest, no update metadata parser, no signature threshold tests, no staged install tests, no tamper replay tests, no partial-write fault tests, no rollback migration tests, no production-key separation review, no production signing keys, no offline root keys, no stable channel, no public binary distribution, no real updater, no real user profile migration, no rollback safety claim, no migration safety claim, no release readiness claim, no supported security claim, no production updater claim, and no implementation claim.

review_scope.prohibited_placeholder_policy: Template records must not leave null fields in a real review or name placeholder lab packages, reference platforms, fixture policies, owner reviewers, independent reviewers, release-operations reviewers, security reviewers, privacy reviewers, title-only approval, self-approval, production-key separation approval, or implied release authority.

package_axes.required_evidence: Executable research package manifest binds source commit, tree status, and build ID with owner review beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.

Executable research package manifest records unique build ID, reproducible command, artifact identity, and retained hashes beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.

Executable manifest proves research-only channel labeling and rejects stable channel, beta promotion, and public distribution beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records supported lab platform and rejects wrong-target platform metadata beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records architecture and rejects wrong-target architecture metadata beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records Rust, Cargo, linker, host shell, and reproducible toolchain identity beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records feature flags and unsupported capability labels beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest references generated SBOM and license/provenance evidence beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records provenance, source archive identity, and build recipe evidence beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records debug-symbol identity, retention, redaction, and access policy beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records notices, license files, and user-visible unsupported status beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records SHA-256 or stronger artifact hashes and rejects mismatch cases beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest records artifact sizes and rejects truncated, oversized, or mismatched targets beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.
Executable manifest displays no-stable-support status in package, metadata, logs, and retained evidence beyond the checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template.

metadata_axes.required_evidence: Fake-key metadata lab proves delegated role separation, key IDs, and refusal of production trust roots.
Signature threshold tests prove under-signed, wrong-key, revoked-key, and mixed-key metadata are rejected.
Expiry tests prove expired metadata, stale targets, and clock-skew cases fail closed.
Minimum secure version tests prove vulnerable version refusal and downgrade blocking.
Rollout tests prove cohort binding, staged activation, wrong-cohort rejection, and hold behavior.
Mirror tests prove hash, size, target, and metadata identity are enforced across local mirror fixtures.
Staged install tests prove temporary root isolation, identity verification, atomic switch, cleanup, and rollback hold behavior.
Tamper tests prove modified metadata or target artifacts are rejected without activation.
Replay tests prove older metadata cannot replace newer accepted metadata or bypass minimum secure version.
Wrong-target tests prove package ID, channel, platform, architecture, feature, and profile-compatibility mismatches are rejected.
Partial-write tests prove interrupted metadata or target writes cannot activate a partial build.
Disk-full tests prove active build preservation, staged artifact cleanup, visible failure, and no data loss.
Power-loss tests prove resumable or safely rejected staged state without corrupting active package or profile compatibility records.

rollback_migration_axes.required_evidence: Rollback tests prove only authorized known-good fake-key targets can be restored and revoked or vulnerable targets are refused.
Vulnerable version refusal tests prove minimum secure version metadata blocks known-bad downgrade and rollback targets.
Migration tests prove package update and profile schema compatibility behavior with fake profiles only.
Downgrade tests prove older builds cannot silently write unsupported profile formats or bypass compatibility holds.
Crash-loop tests prove staged build hold, rollback candidate validation, and visible recovery without release-channel claims.
Profile compatibility tests prove fake-profile migration boundaries and no real user profile migration.
Local event records use IDs, hashes, and reason codes without profile contents, credentials, private-session data, raw paths, or secrets.

fixture_policy_axes.required_evidence: Fake signing keys are generated, scoped to the lab, retained only as permitted, and never confused with production signing keys.
fixture_policy_axes.template_status: required but not provided in template

fixture_policy_axes.required_evidence: Production signing keys, hardware-backed release keys, and offline root keys are absent and rejected by the review.
Offline root keys remain prohibited and any offline-root ceremony is outside this review scope.
Metadata is served from bounded local lab paths only, with no public network distribution or stable-channel service.
Fake profiles and fake migration data are used for all update, rollback, crash-loop, and privacy-event tests.
Temporary roots are bounded, isolated, destroyed after tests, and cannot point at production install or profile directories.
Cleanup evidence proves fake keys, fake profiles, staged artifacts, local metadata, and temp roots are removed or retained by explicit policy.

security_release_axes.required_evidence: Security and release-operations reviewers accept evidence that production signing keys and offline roots were absent.
Release readiness remains rejected until owner-reviewed production signing, update trust, channel, support, and rollback evidence exists.
Supported security remains rejected until vulnerability response, supported lifecycle, update availability, and owner review exist.
Stable channel remains prohibited until separate promotion policy, signing authority, update service, and support commitments exist.
Public binary distribution remains prohibited until packaging, signing, legal, support, and incident-response gates close.
Real updater implementation remains out of scope until executable lab evidence and owner-reviewed update trust are accepted.
Production updater remains rejected until production keys, stable channel, rollout, rollback, incident response, and support gates are approved.
Human release authority, legal approval, signing authority, and two-person control remain owner-only decisions outside this template.

owner_review_axes.required_evidence: Release-operations owner accepts package identity, update metadata, rollback, migration, channel, and unsupported boundaries.
Security owner accepts fake-key trust model, signature threshold, expiry, tamper, replay, wrong-target, and key-separation evidence.
Storage owner accepts profile compatibility, downgrade, fake-profile migration, rollback, and no real user profile migration boundaries.
Privacy-data reviewer accepts local event minimization, redaction, retention, and no profile contents or credentials.
Quality reviewer accepts tamper, replay, wrong-target, expiry, partial-write, disk-full, power-loss, rollback, downgrade, and crash-loop tests.
Production-readiness reviewer keeps release readiness, supported security, public distribution, and production updater claims blocked until owner evidence exists.
Legal and human-release reviewers accept or reject distribution, signing, support, and release-authority implications outside the template.
Documentation, readiness, task, crosswalk, risks, requirements, repository map, and validation records are updated after review.

rejection_rules.condition: This checked no-claim research package/update readiness-review template is cited as completed readiness evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject release readiness, supported security, production updater, stable channel, public distribution, signing readiness, and implementation claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Lab package, reference platform, fixture policy, owner reviewer, independent reviewer, release-operations reviewer, security reviewer, or privacy reviewer is null, placeholder, title-only, self-approved, or unqualified.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner review, release-operations review, security review, privacy review, and readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Executable package manifest generation and validation are missing or based only on this template.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject package/update readiness and release readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Update metadata parser, role separation, signature threshold, expiry, minimum secure version, rollout, mirror, and target-binding tests are missing.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject update readiness and production updater claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Production signing keys, offline root keys, hardware-backed release keys, or real update trust roots are used in lab fixtures.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject lab evidence, key-separation review, and release readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Stable channel, beta promotion, public distribution, real updater, or release-readiness behavior is claimed from this template or fake-key lab evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject release-channel, production updater, supported-security, and public distribution claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Real user profile data, real credentials, private-session data, production profile roots, or real user migration evidence are used before owner-approved fixture policy exists.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject migration safety, rollback safety, privacy, and user-data claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Tamper, replay, wrong-target, expired, mirror-mismatched, under-signed, wrong-key, revoked, or vulnerable-version cases are reported as successful updates.
Partial-write, disk-full, or power-loss cases activate staged artifacts, corrupt active state, hide cleanup failure, or report success.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject rollback safety, migration safety, and release readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Rollback safety, migration safety, or data-loss safety is claimed without authorized known-good rollback, vulnerable-version refusal, migration, downgrade, crash-loop, profile compatibility, and privacy-event evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject rollback, migration, data-loss, and release claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Local event records include profile contents, credentials, private-session data, secrets, raw user paths, or unredacted diagnostics.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject privacy review and package/update readiness claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Release-operations, security, storage, privacy-data, quality, production-readiness, legal, or human-release review is missing, stale, self-approved, or not tied to retained evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject owner-reviewed readiness, supported security, release readiness, and production updater claims.

rejection_rules.condition: Passing repository validation, package/update lab validation, or a no-claim template is cited as proof of release readiness, supported security, production updater readiness, or implementation completion.
A document removes unsupported behavior, residual risk, claim boundary, no-claim wording, or prohibited claim language without replacement evidence.
rejection_rules.outcome: Reject the change and keep PB-017 partial.
